title: Fix randomize() on null object handle crashing instead of returning 0
body:
## Summary

Calling `randomize()` on a null class object handle causes a runtime abort ("Null pointer dereferenced") instead of returning 0 (failure). Per IEEE 1800-2017 Section 18.4, `randomize()` should return 0 on failure, including when the handle is null. QuestaSim correctly returns 0 in this scenario.

## Current Behavior

- `obj.randomize()` where `obj` is null crashes with:
%Error: test.sv:24: Null pointer dereferenced
Aborting...



## Expected Behavior

- `obj.randomize()` on a null handle returns 0 without aborting
- Subsequent code can check the return value and handle the null case gracefully

## Source

- planvtech/PlanV_Verilator_Feature_Tests#63

---
PR is coming.
